description: 4 domains x 3 sequential, thematically-evolving queries (12 tasks total), designed specifically to test query-family memory compounding (VP9) -- unlike task_set_v1.json, whose 18 tasks are each one-shot and structurally cannot exercise memory. Each domain's 3 queries simulate a real user progressively researching the same company: (1) basic identity/contact, (2) competitive landscape, (3) partnership/relationship signals -- all accumulating query-family memory in the same domain. Ground truth for query 1 and 3 in the TSMC/MediaTek/CNCF/Advantech domains is reused from task_set_v1.json's already-verified research (same entities, same session) rather than re-derived from scratch; query 2's ground truth (competitors) was freshly researched via WebSearch for this task set and is cited below.

ground_truth_entities.name: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company Limited
ground_truth_entities.note: Reused from task_set_v1.json single-01-tsmc (same session, already verified).

ground_truth_entities.name: Samsung Foundry
ground_truth_entities.relationship: #2 foundry by market share (~7.2% in 2025), 3nm process rollout

ground_truth_entities.name: Intel Foundry
ground_truth_entities.relationship: Sovereign-manufacturing-focused competitor, not yet in top-10 foundry ranking by revenue

ground_truth_entities.name: SMIC (Semiconductor Manufacturing International Corporation)
ground_truth_entities.relationship: #3 foundry by market share (~5.32% in 2025)

ground_truth_entities.name: UMC (United Microelectronics Corporation)
ground_truth_entities.relationship: #4 foundry by market share (~4.35% in 2025), mature-node specialist

ground_truth_entities.name: GlobalFoundries
ground_truth_entities.relationship: #5 foundry by market share (~3.87% in 2025), mature-node and specialty market

sequence.notes: TSMC itself holds ~70% of Q3 2025 foundry revenue, ~10x more than the next competitor -- included for context, not as a competitor entry.

ground_truth_entities.relationship: Largest customer by revenue share

ground_truth_entities.relationship: Major AI GPU customer, reportedly overtaking Apple as largest customer 2025-2026

ground_truth_entities.name: AMD
ground_truth_entities.relationship: Advanced-node CPU/GPU customer

ground_truth_entities.name: MediaTek
ground_truth_entities.relationship: Flagship mobile SoC customer

ground_truth_entities.name: MediaTek Inc.
ground_truth_entities.note: Reused from task_set_v1.json single-04-mediatek (same session, already verified).

ground_truth_entities.name: Qualcomm
ground_truth_entities.relationship: Primary premium-SoC competitor; MediaTek 32% vs Qualcomm 23% smartphone chipset share, Q1 2026

ground_truth_entities.name: Unisoc
ground_truth_entities.relationship: Low-end/entry-5G competitor pressuring MediaTek's volume in Africa and South Asia

ground_truth_entities.name: Apple (A-series/M-series silicon)
ground_truth_entities.relationship: Exclusive to Apple devices, sets performance benchmark but not a direct chipset-market rival (no external sales)

sequence.notes: Both MediaTek and Qualcomm targeting 2nm flagship launches (Dimensity 9600 / Snapdragon 8 Elite Gen 6) in H2 2026 per the same source.

ground_truth_entities.name: Samsung
ground_truth_entities.relationship: Dimensity chipsets in Galaxy A-series/Tab S; Samsung validated LPDDR5X for MediaTek's flagship platform

intent.industry: cloud native / open source software foundation

ground_truth_entities.name: Cloud Native Computing Foundation
ground_truth_entities.note: Reused from task_set_v1.json single-03-cncf (same session, already verified).

ground_truth_entities.name: The Linux Foundation
ground_truth_entities.relationship: CNCF is a SUBSIDIARY of the Linux Foundation, not a competing/peer foundation -- this is deliberately a different relationship shape than mem-tsmc-02/mem-mediatek-02's 'competitors' framing

sequence.notes: DELIBERATE DESIGN CHOICE: this query intentionally does NOT ask for 'competitors' like the TSMC/MediaTek domains, because CNCF genuinely has no direct competing-foundation relationship -- it's organizationally part of the Linux Foundation. Framed as 'parent organization' instead, both to get a real, verifiable answer and to test whether an agent (or Salva) honestly reports a parent/subsidiary relationship rather than forcing a 'competitors' answer where none exists, or fabricating rival foundations. A confidently-wrong 'CNCF competes with OpenStack Foundation' answer would be a red flag, not a correct one.

ground_truth_entities.name: Kontron
ground_truth_entities.relationship: Primary rival, high-performance 4U platforms for energy/medical/defense, HQ Ismaning Bavaria

ground_truth_entities.name: ADLINK Technology
ground_truth_entities.relationship: Taiwan-based competitor, embedded computing and automation for retail/automotive

ground_truth_entities.name: AAEON
ground_truth_entities.relationship: Competitor, industrial computers/panel PCs/embedded boards/single board computers

sequence.notes: Advantech itself holds ~41% of the global Industrial PC market per the same research pass, well ahead of these competitors -- included for context, not as a competitor entry. Siemens and Beckhoff Automation mentioned as other regional players but with less direct source corroboration than the top 3 -- excluded from ground truth to avoid padding.
